schemeSetu.description: Comprehensive collection of Central Government and Punjab State Government schemes for age groups 6 to 45 years. Updated with amount and deadline fields.
schemeSetu.changelog: v3.0 — Added 'amount' (single string format) and 'deadline' fields to all 55 schemes.

schemes.name: Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan
schemes.amount: Free textbooks + uniforms; transport allowance up to ₹6,000/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — enrollment open throughout academic year
schemes.description: An integrated umbrella scheme for school education covering pre-primary to Class XII. Merges Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, RMSA, and Teacher Education into one comprehensive program for quality and inclusive education.

eligibility.residency: All Indian students in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on marginalized groups, children with disabilities, and girls

financial: Free uniforms for girls and SC/ST students in Classes 1 to 8
Transport allowance up to ₹6,000/year for remote area children
Stipend of ₹200/month for girls with disabilities in Classes 9 to 12

howToApply: Step 1: Enroll child in the nearest government or government-aided school
Step 2: Register with school office with required documents
Step 3: Benefits like uniforms and textbooks are distributed automatically through school
Step 4: For transport allowance, apply via the District Education Office
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of child and parent

schemes.name: PM POSHAN Scheme (Mid-Day Meal)
schemes.amount: Free hot meal daily; ₹6.19/day (primary), ₹9.29/day (upper primary)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — benefit provided on all school days automatically
schemes.description: One of the world's largest school feeding programs providing hot-cooked nutritious meals to students in government and government-aided schools daily to improve nutrition and boost attendance.

eligibility.residency: Students enrolled in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers Madrasas supported under Samagra Shiksha

financial: Free hot-cooked meal every school day
Meal cost: ₹6.19/day per child for primary; ₹9.29/day for upper primary (from Dec 2024)
nonFinancial: Improved nutrition and reduced classroom hunger
Reduction in malnutrition among school children

howToApply: Step 1: Enroll child in a government or government-aided school
Step 2: Meal is automatically provided on all school days — no separate application needed

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: Linked to SSY — 8.2% interest/year; min deposit ₹250/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — accounts can be opened anytime for girls below 10 years
schemes.description: A national campaign to address declining Child Sex Ratio and promote education and empowerment of the girl child by preventing female foeticide and improving enrollment of girls in schools.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on districts with low child sex ratio

financial: Linked to Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana with 8.2% interest per annum
nonFinancial: Support for girl child school enrollment

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest post office or authorized bank
Step 2: Open a Sukanya Samriddhi Account for the girl child
Step 3: Submit required documents
Step 4: Deposit minimum ₹250 to activate account

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.amount: Min ₹250/year to max ₹1,50,000/year; 8.2% interest per annum
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open for girls below 10 years at any time
schemes.description: A small savings scheme for the girl child offering one of the highest interest rates. Part of BBBP, it encourages parents to save for their daughter's education and marriage.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 accounts per family; exception for twins/triplets

financial: Interest rate of 8.2% per annum (compounded annually)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any post office or authorized bank
Step 2: Fill SSY account opening form
Step 3: Submit documents and make initial deposit
Step 4: Collect passbook

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
schemes.amount: ₹12,000/year (₹1,000/month) for Classes 9 to 12
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open July/August each year; exam in Nov–Feb (state-wise)
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scholarship for meritorious students from low-income families to reduce dropout at Class 8 and encourage continuation of education at secondary level.

eligibility.otherCriteria: 55% in Class 7 (50% for SC/ST); family income below ₹3.5 lakh/year; government school

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Appear for State-level NMMS Exam conducted by SCERT
Step 3: Selected students receive scholarship upon verification
Step 4: Renew each year by submitting progress report

schemes.name: National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
schemes.amount: ₹3,000 fixed deposit (withdrawable with interest after Class 10 and age 18)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply through school after passing Class 8
schemes.description: Provides financial incentive to SC/ST girl students passing Class 8 to encourage enrollment in secondary school and reduce girl child dropouts.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST; passed Class 8; government school; unmarried

financial: ₹3,000 in FD account; withdrawable with interest after age 18 and Class 10

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through school principal after passing Class 8
Step 2: School submits eligible girls list to District Education Officer
Step 3: Verified girls get FD account opened in their name

schemes.name: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV)
schemes.amount: Free residential education — accommodation, meals, uniforms, books (no cost to student)
schemes.deadline: Annual — admissions at start of academic year (April–June); contact local Block Education Office
schemes.description: Residential schools for girls from marginalized communities in educationally backward blocks, ensuring quality education and safe accommodation for girls aged 10 to 18.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC, ST, OBC, minority, BPL girls; dropout girls

howToApply: Step 1: Contact nearest KGBV school or Block Education Office
Step 2: Submit application form with documents
Step 3: Selection done by district authorities based on eligibility

schemes.name: Mission Poshan 2.0 (Saksham Anganwadi)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: Free supplementary nutrition, health check-ups, and ECCE (no monetary benefit)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register at Anganwadi Centre anytime
schemes.description: Integrated nutrition support to address stunting, wasting, and anemia among children 0–6 years and adolescent girls 14–18, delivered through Anganwadi Centres.

eligibility.ageLimit: Children 6 months to 6 years; Adolescent girls 14 to 18 years
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers pregnant and lactating mothers

nonFinancial: Iron, Vitamin B12, Folic Acid supplements

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Anganwadi Centre
Step 2: Register child with Anganwadi worker
Step 3: Benefits provided automatically after enrollment

schemes.name: PM CARES for Children
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: ₹10 lakh corpus by age 18 + ₹20,000 school scholarship + ₹5 lakh health insurance
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at pmcaresforchildren.in
schemes.description: Comprehensive support for children who lost parents due to COVID-19, providing education, health insurance, and financial support until age 23.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Both parents or guardian died due to COVID-19 from March 11, 2020

nonFinancial: ₹5 lakh health insurance under Ayushman Bharat

howToApply: Step 1: Visit pmcaresforchildren.in
Step 2: Register as new applicant
Step 3: Upload COVID-19 death certificate and child documents
Step 4: District Magistrate verifies and approves

schemes.name: Right to Education (RTE) Act
schemes.amount: Free education (tuition + books) in government schools; free seats in 25% private school quota
schemes.deadline: Annual — RTE admission season is typically March–May each year
schemes.description: A landmark law ensuring free and compulsory education to all children aged 6 to 14. Mandates private schools to reserve 25% of seats for EWS/disadvantaged children.

eligibility.otherCriteria: EWS/disadvantaged children for 25% private school quota

financial: Completely free education in government schools
Free education in private schools under 25% quota for EWS

howToApply: Step 1: For government school — directly enroll at nearest government school
Step 2: For private school 25% quota — apply through District Education Office or state RTE portal
Step 3: Submit income and address proof
Step 4: Admission granted after verification

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC Students
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice / Tribal Affairs
schemes.amount: ₹225/month (day scholar) or ₹525/month (hosteller) for Classes 9–10
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC students in Classes 9 and 10 to reduce dropout rates and encourage continuation of secondary education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST/OBC; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; government school

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on NSP at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill personal, bank, and school details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: School verifies and forwards to district

schemes.name: Balika Samriddhi Yojana (BSY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: ₹500 at birth + scholarships ₹300 to ₹1,000/year (Class 1 to 10)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply within 1 year of birth at nearest Anganwadi Centre
schemes.description: Supports girl children from BPL families with birth grant and annual scholarships to improve their status and encourage education up to Class 10.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 girls per family; must be enrolled in school

financial: Annual scholarships from Class 1 to 10 (₹300 to ₹1,000/year)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Anganwadi worker or ICDS office
Step 2: Fill BSY application form
Step 3: Submit documents for verification
Step 4: Bank account opened in girl's name; scholarship credited annually
documentsRequired: BPL ration card or income certificate

schemes.name: NPS Vatsalya Scheme
schemes.amount: Contribution-based; min ₹1,000/year; grows via compounding until age 18
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open at any PFRDA-registered Point of Presence anytime
schemes.description: A pension savings scheme for minors allowing parents to open and contribute to an NPS account until the child turns 18, when it seamlessly converts to a regular NPS account.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Managed by parent or legal guardian

financial: Tax benefits similar to regular NPS
Converts to regular NPS at age 18

howToApply: Step 1: Visit PFRDA-registered PoP (bank or post office)
Step 2: Fill NPS Vatsalya account opening form
Step 3: Submit documents
Step 4: Make initial contribution and receive PRAN

schemes.name: Bebe Nanki Laadli Beti Kalyan Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹20,000 deposited with LIC; total payout of ₹61,000 across 5 milestones up to age 18
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply within 1 year of girl's birth at nearest Anganwadi or DSS office
schemes.description: Punjab scheme to curb female foeticide by providing tiered financial assistance to girl children from birth to age 18 through LIC-backed disbursement.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Includes abandoned girls in Punjab orphanages born after Jan 1, 2011

financial: Total ₹61,000 disbursed at milestones: birth, school admission, Class 8, Class 10, age 18

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Anganwadi or District Social Security Office in Punjab
Step 2: Fill application form
Step 3: Submit documents for verification
Step 4: Amount deposited with LIC in girl's name

schemes.name: Mai Bhago Vidya Scheme (Free Bicycle)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: 1 free bicycle per eligible girl student (no monetary value transferred)
schemes.deadline: Annual — distribution happens at start of academic year (April–June) through school
schemes.description: Encourages girl students in Classes 9 to 12 in Punjab government schools to continue education by providing free bicycles, reducing commute barriers.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Enrolled in government school in Punjab

nonFinancial: Free bicycle per eligible girl student

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through school where girl is enrolled
Step 2: School prepares and submits eligible girls list to District Education Office
Step 3: Bicycles distributed at school level

schemes.name: Punjab Ashirwad (Shagun) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹51,000 one-time financial assistance per girl for marriage
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply before the marriage date at District Social Security Office
schemes.description: Provides ₹51,000 to EWS families for marriage of their girl child to reduce financial burden and prevent child marriage.

eligibility.ageLimit: Girl must be 18 years or above at marriage
eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL/SC/ST/OBC families; widows/divorced women for daughters; orphan girls

financial: ₹51,000 financial assistance per girl for marriage

howToApply: Step 1: Visit District Social Security Office in Punjab
Step 2: Fill Ashirwad Scheme form
Step 3: Submit documents before marriage date
Step 4: Amount deposited directly in applicant's account
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of girl and parents

schemes.name: Scholarship for Students with Disabilities (Punjab)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹200/month (Class 1–8); ₹300/month (Class 9 and above)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at District Social Security Office anytime during academic year
schemes.description: 100% state-funded monthly scholarship for students with disabilities in Punjab schools to support their education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have recognized disability certificate; enrolled in Punjab school

howToApply: Step 1: Obtain disability certificate from CMO
Step 2: Visit District Social Security Office
Step 3: Fill application and submit documents
Step 4: Monthly scholarship credited after verification
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of student and parent

schemes.name: PM YASASVI Scholarship (OBC/EBC/DNT)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.deadline: Annual — YET exam registration opens around July; exam usually in September
schemes.description: PM Young Achievers Scholarship for OBC/EBC/DNT students in Classes 9 and 11 — one of India's highest school-level scholarships by amount.

eligibility.otherCriteria: OBC/EBC/DNT; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; must qualify YASASVI Entrance Test (YET)

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on NSP at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Appear for YASASVI Entrance Test (YET) conducted by NTA
Step 3: Selected students notified; scholarship credited via DBT

schemes.name: AICTE Swanath Scholarship
schemes.amount: ₹50,000/year for the full course duration (max 4 years degree / 3 years diploma)
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open around August–October each year on AICTE portal
schemes.description: Supports orphan students, COVID-19 orphans, and wards of martyred armed forces personnel pursuing technical education in AICTE-approved institutions.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Orphan/COVID-19 orphan/ward of martyred armed forces; income below ₹8 lakh/year; AICTE-approved institution

howToApply: Step 1: Apply through AICTE portal at aicte-india.org
Step 2: Fill Swanath Scholarship form
Step 3: Upload supporting documents
Step 4: Institution verifies; AICTE releases scholarship
documentsRequired: Proof of orphan status / COVID death certificate

schemes.name: Punjab Chief Minister's Scholarship Scheme
schemes.amount: Tuition fee waiver + monthly stipend (varies by education level)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on scholarships.punjab.gov.in; deadline typically October 31 each year
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance and tuition fee waiver to meritorious students from EWS in Punjab studying in government institutions.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Meritorious EWS students in government institutions in Punjab

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at scholarships.punjab.gov.in
Step 2: Fill personal and academic details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: School/college verifies and forwards

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY 4.0)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
schemes.amount: Free skill training + average monetary reward of ₹8,000 upon certification
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions at PMKVY Training Centres throughout the year
schemes.description: India's flagship free skill training scheme for youth to improve employability with industry-relevant certification and placement support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: School/college dropouts and unemployed youth given priority

financial: Average monetary reward of ₹8,000 upon certification
nonFinancial: Soft skills and digital literacy training

howToApply: Step 1: Visit skillindiadigital.gov.in or call helpline
Step 2: Find nearby PMKVY Training Centre
Step 3: Register at centre with documents
Step 4: Complete training; appear for assessment and receive certification

schemes.name: PM Internship Scheme (PMIS)
schemes.amount: ₹5,000/month allowance + ₹6,000 one-time assistance
schemes.deadline: Annual rounds — Round 1 closed March 2025; Round 2 expected mid-2025; check pminternship.mca.gov.in
schemes.description: Provides 1 crore youth with 12-month internships in top 500 Indian companies with monthly allowance and hands-on industry experience.

eligibility.residency: Indian citizen; not in full-time education
eligibility.otherCriteria: ITI/diploma/BA/BSc/BCom graduates; family income below ₹8 lakh/year; no IIT/IIM/NLU degrees

howToApply: Step 1: Register at pminternship.mca.gov.in
Step 2: Fill educational and personal details
Step 3: Apply to internship openings in top companies
Step 4: Complete mandatory skill training during internship

schemes.name: Startup India Initiative
schemes.amount: Tax exemption for 3 years + 80% patent fee concession + access to ₹10,000 crore Fund of Funds
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply for DPIIT recognition anytime at startupindia.gov.in
schemes.description: India's flagship entrepreneurship program providing recognition, tax benefits, funding support, and regulatory simplification for startups.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Startup less than 10 years old; turnover below ₹100 crore; scalable business model

financial: Tax exemption on profits for 3 years (Section 80-IAC)
Capital gains tax exemption for investors
Fund of Funds access (₹10,000 crore corpus)

howToApply: Step 1: Register at startupindia.gov.in
Step 2: Submit business plan and incorporation documents
Step 3: Apply for DPIIT recognition
Step 4: Access tax benefits and funding support

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
schemes.amount: Shishu: up to ₹50,000 | Kishore: ₹50,001–₹5 lakh | Tarun: ₹5–₹10 lakh | Tarun Plus: up to ₹20 lakh
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at any bank, MFI, or NBFC anytime
schemes.description: Collateral-free micro-enterprise loans for non-farm income-generating activities for youth and entrepreneurs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Non-farm micro/small enterprise; not a wilful defaulter

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any bank, MFI, or NBFC offering Mudra loans
Step 2: Fill Mudra loan application form
Step 3: Submit business plan and documents
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship (SC Students)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.amount: ₹380–₹1,200/month maintenance allowance + full tuition fee reimbursement
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Financial assistance to SC students beyond Class 10 in recognized institutions to enable completion of education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Scheduled Caste; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

financial: Full tuition fee and non-refundable charges reimbursed

howToApply: Step 1: Register on NSP at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill personal, academic, and bank details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institution verifies; state disburses scholarship

schemes.name: Central Sector Scholarship for College & University Students
schemes.amount: ₹12,000/year (first 3 years); ₹20,000/year (4th and 5th year)
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open on NSP around October 31 each year
schemes.description: Merit-based scholarship for students above 80th percentile in Class 12 from low-income families pursuing higher education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Above 80th percentile in Class 12; family income below ₹4.5 lakh/year; not availing other scholarships

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on NSP at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Fill Class 12 board, income, college details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institute verifies; scholarship credited to bank account

schemes.name: PM-Vidyalakshmi Education Loan Scheme
schemes.amount: Loan up to ₹10 lakh (collateral-free); 3% interest subvention for income below ₹8 lakh/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at vidyalakshmi.co.in after college admission
schemes.description: Centralized education loan portal providing collateral-free loans for quality higher education with government guarantee.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Admitted to QHEI; income below ₹8 lakh preferred; not availing other government schemes

financial: 3% interest subvention during moratorium (income below ₹8 lakh)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit vidyalakshmi.co.in
Step 2: Register and fill personal and academic details
Step 3: Apply to eligible banks offering education loans
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan

schemes.name: Agnipath / Agniveer Scheme
schemes.amount: ₹30,000–₹40,000/month salary + ₹11.71 lakh tax-free Seva Nidhi at exit
schemes.deadline: Annual recruitment cycles — check joinindianarmy.nic.in for current notification dates
schemes.description: Short-term 4-year military service scheme for youth with financial benefits, skills, and 25% chance of permanent enrollment.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must meet physical and educational requirements for respective service branch

financial: ₹11.71 lakh tax-free Seva Nidhi at exit

howToApply: Step 1: Watch for recruitment notifications on joinindianarmy.nic.in
Step 2: Apply online during notification period
Step 3: Appear for written exam and physical fitness test
Step 4: Attend medical examination
Step 5: Join training on appointment letter

schemes.name: National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS)
schemes.amount: ₹8,000–₹9,000/month stipend (employer + government contribution of ₹1,500/month)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at nats.education.gov.in; openings depend on company
schemes.description: Practical 1–2 year apprenticeship training for engineering/diploma graduates in government and private organizations.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Engineering or diploma graduates from AICTE-approved institutions

howToApply: Step 1: Register on NATS portal at nats.education.gov.in
Step 2: Search apprenticeship openings by sector
Step 3: Apply for relevant opportunities
Step 4: Attend interview and sign apprenticeship agreement

schemes.name: National Youth Corps (NYC) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
schemes.amount: ₹5,000/month honorarium during volunteer service
schemes.deadline: Annual — contact nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra (NYK) for current enrollment cycle
schemes.description: Builds disciplined youth volunteers contributing to community development, disaster relief, and environmental awareness.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed; at least matriculation qualification

nonFinancial: Service certificate for government job applications
Training in community service and disaster management

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra (NYK)
Step 2: Fill NYC application form
Step 3: Submit documents and attend selection
Step 4: Deploy in community service activities

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.amount: 25–45% government subsidy on project cost; max project ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (services)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
schemes.description: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for micro-enterprise setup in non-farm sector with government margin money subsidy.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Minimum 8th standard for projects above ₹10 lakh in manufacturing

financial: Project cost up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (services)

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
Step 2: Submit project and personal details to KVIC/KVIB/DIC
Step 3: Interview by task force committee
Step 4: Bank sanctions loan; government releases subsidy

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
schemes.amount: Free training + placement with min ₹6,000/month salary guarantee
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions at DDU-GKY training centres; contact DRDA
schemes.description: Free market-linked skill training for rural poor youth with mandatory placement support in formal sector.

eligibility.ageLimit: 15 to 35 years (45 for women and vulnerable groups)
eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 families; priority to SC/ST, women, PWD

financial: Free training + placement with minimum ₹6,000/month formal sector salary
nonFinancial: Free accommodation and meals during training

howToApply: Step 1: Contact nearest DRDA or DDU-GKY training centre
Step 2: Register and fill application
Step 3: Complete skill training (3 to 12 months)
Step 4: Attend placement drives

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.amount: Loan of ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore per borrower for greenfield enterprise
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at standupmitra.in or nearest scheduled bank branch
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs for setting up greenfield enterprises.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Greenfield enterprise; not a bank defaulter

financial: Repayment up to 7 years; moratorium up to 18 months

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at standupmitra.in
Step 2: Register and fill business plan
Step 3: Connect with Loan Facilitation Centre or bank
Step 4: Bank processes and disburses loan

schemes.name: Punjab Skill Development Mission (PSDM)
schemes.ministry: Department of Technical Education and Industrial Training, Punjab
schemes.amount: Free skill training courses + stipend during training (for eligible candidates)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions at PSDM centres; visit psdm.gov.in for current batches
schemes.description: State-level skill training mission in Punjab to equip youth with relevant industry skills and connect them to employment.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Priority to unemployed youth, school dropouts, marginalized communities

financial: Free skill training + stipend during training (for eligible candidates)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit psdm.gov.in or nearest PSDM centre in Punjab
Step 2: Register online or at centre
Step 3: Choose skill course
Step 4: Complete training and appear for assessment

schemes.name: Aam Aadmi Rozgar Yojana (AARY) – Punjab
schemes.amount: Free job portal + free skill courses + access to job fairs (no direct monetary benefit)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at pgrkam.com
schemes.description: Punjab scheme to provide employment opportunities to youth through job fairs, skill training, and entrepreneurship support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed graduates and diploma holders registered in Punjab employment exchange

howToApply: Step 1: Register at pgrkam.com
Step 2: Create profile with education and skills
Step 3: Browse jobs and register for job fairs
Step 4: Attend job fairs and interviews

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Sehat Bima Yojana (MMSBY) – Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹10 lakh cashless health insurance per family per year (zero premium for beneficiary)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — enroll at any Aam Aadmi Clinic or government hospital in Punjab
schemes.description: Punjab's flagship cashless health insurance covering 100% of Punjab's population for up to ₹10 lakh per family per year.

eligibility.otherCriteria: All Punjab residents covered (100% population coverage)

financial: ₹10 lakh cashless insurance per family per year

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Aam Aadmi Clinic or government hospital
Step 2: Register with Aadhaar card for MMSBY health card
Step 3: Use health card for cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities
schemes.amount: Course fee up to ₹20,000/year + maintenance allowance ₹10,000/year
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Financial assistance to students from minority communities pursuing professional/technical courses.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi; 50% marks in previous year; income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

howToApply: Step 1: Apply on NSP at scholarships.gov.in
Step 2: Select Minority Scholarship and fill details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institute verifies; Ministry releases scholarship

schemes.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls (Technical Education)
schemes.amount: ₹50,000/year (up to 4 years degree / 3 years diploma)
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open August–October on AICTE portal
schemes.description: Supports girl students from low-income families in AICTE-approved technical institutions to encourage girls in technical fields.

eligibility.ageLimit: First and second year diploma or degree students
eligibility.otherCriteria: Income below ₹8 lakh/year; one girl per family per year; AICTE-approved institution

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at aicte-india.org
Step 2: Fill personal, academic, and bank details
Step 3: Upload documents
Step 4: Institution verifies; AICTE releases annually

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Interest subsidy of 3–6.5% on home loan (EWS: 6.5% on ₹6 lakh; MIG-I: 4% on ₹9 lakh; MIG-II: 3% on ₹12 lakh)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing under PMAY 2.0 — apply at pmaymis.gov.in or nearest ULB
schemes.description: Affordable housing for urban EWS/LIG/MIG households with interest subsidies and direct housing construction support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: No pucca house anywhere; EWS (income ≤₹3 lakh), LIG (₹3–6 lakh), MIG-I (₹6–12 lakh), MIG-II (₹12–18 lakh)

financial: EWS/LIG: 6.5% interest subsidy on loan up to ₹6 lakh

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at pmaymis.gov.in or nearest ULB/CSC
Step 2: Fill personal, income, property details
Step 3: Submit documents
Step 4: Interest subsidy credited upfront to reduce EMI
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of applicant and spouse

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.amount: ₹1,20,000 (plains) or ₹1,30,000 (hilly areas) + ₹12,000 for toilet under SBM
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — eligibility based on SECC 2011 waitlist; contact Gram Panchayat
schemes.description: Rural housing scheme providing financial assistance to BPL households to construct pucca houses.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 identified; houseless or kutcha house

financial: ₹12,000 for toilet under Swachh Bharat Mission

howToApply: Step 1: Contact Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office
Step 2: Check if name is in SECC 2011 waitlist
Step 3: District officials verify and include in beneficiary list
Step 4: Funds transferred in instalments based on construction progress

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
schemes.amount: Zero balance account + ₹2 lakh accidental insurance + ₹30,000 life insurance + ₹10,000 overdraft
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open account at any bank branch or Business Correspondent anytime
schemes.description: Universal banking access with zero balance accounts, free accidental insurance, overdraft facility, and access to pension and insurance schemes.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unbanked individuals

howToApply: Step 1: Visit any bank or Business Correspondent
Step 2: Fill PMJDY account opening form
Step 3: Submit documents
Step 4: Receive passbook and RuPay debit card
documentsRequired: OR: Voter ID, Driving License, NREGA Job Card

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.amount: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover at only ₹436/year premium
schemes.deadline: Annual renewal — auto-debited on June 1 each year; enroll anytime
schemes.description: Government-backed term life insurance providing ₹2 lakh on death at a nominal annual premium of ₹436.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have bank account with auto-debit

financial: ₹2 lakh life cover on death (any cause)

howToApply: Step 1: Visit bank or apply via net/mobile banking
Step 2: Submit consent for auto-debit
Step 3: Policy activates from June 1

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.amount: ₹2 lakh on accidental death/full disability; ₹1 lakh on partial disability — at just ₹20/year
schemes.deadline: Annual renewal — auto-debited on June 1; enroll anytime at your bank
schemes.description: Very low-cost accidental insurance at ₹20/year providing ₹2 lakh cover for accidental death and disability.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have bank account

financial: ₹2 lakh on accidental death or permanent total disability

howToApply: Step 1: Visit bank or apply via net/mobile banking
Step 2: Fill PMSBY enrollment form
Step 3: Premium auto-debited annually

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
schemes.amount: ₹5 lakh cashless health insurance per family per year (zero premium for beneficiary)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — check eligibility and get Ayushman card at any empanelled hospital or pmjay.gov.in
schemes.description: World's largest government health insurance scheme providing ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 identified families; recently extended to all senior citizens above 70 years

financial: ₹5 lakh cashless insurance per family per year

howToApply: Step 1: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or call 14555
Step 2: Visit empanelled hospital or Ayushman Bharat Mitra
Step 3: Get Ayushman Golden Card with Aadhaar
Step 4: Use card for cashless treatment
documentsRequired: Ration card or SECC inclusion proof

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemes.amount: Free LPG connection (cylinder + regulator + hose); continued subsidy via PAHAL
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at nearest LPG distributor anytime
schemes.description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households to replace polluting cooking fuels.

eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL/SECC/SC/ST/forest area/PMAY/PMJDY/tea garden workers

financial: Free LPG connection with 14.2 kg cylinder, regulator, hose

howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest LPG distributor
Step 2: Fill KYC form for new LPG connection
Step 3: Submit BPL/SECC certificate
Step 4: Receive free gas connection

schemes.name: MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee)
schemes.amount: 100 days guaranteed employment/year at ₹220–₹357/day (state-wise wage rate)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply for Job Card at Gram Panchayat anytime; work allocation within 15 days
schemes.description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Any rural adult willing to do unskilled manual work; Job Card required

financial: Unemployment allowance if work not given in 15 days
nonFinancial: Crèche, first aid, and drinking water at worksite

howToApply: Step 1: Register at nearest Gram Panchayat
Step 2: Receive MGNREGS Job Card
Step 3: Submit written work application at Gram Panchayat
Step 4: Work allocated within 15 days; payment within 15 days of work
documentsRequired: Aadhaar card of all adult members

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.amount: Full sum insured for total crop loss; farmer pays only 2% (Kharif), 1.5% (Rabi), 5% (horticulture) premium
schemes.deadline: Seasonal — Kharif: apply by July 31; Rabi: apply by December 31 (check state-wise dates)
schemes.description: Comprehensive crop insurance against natural calamities, pests, and diseases for financial stability of farmers.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Loanee and non-loanee farmers; sharecroppers and tenant farmers also eligible

financial: Full sum insured for total crop loss
Low farmer premium: 2% Kharif, 1.5% Rabi, 5% horticulture
Balance premium borne by Central and State Government

howToApply: Step 1: Contact nearest bank, CSC, or insurance company agent
Step 2: Fill crop insurance application before cut-off date
Step 3: Pay applicable premium
Step 4: Claim settled based on crop cutting experiments and satellite data
documentsRequired: Land ownership records or rental agreement

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.amount: ₹6,000/year in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 every 4 months via DBT
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at pmkisan.gov.in; instalments released every 4 months
schemes.description: Direct income support of ₹6,000/year to all eligible farmer families in 3 instalments to cover cultivation costs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Small and marginal farmers with arable land; excludes institutional landholders, government employees, income tax payers

financial: Direct credit to Aadhaar-linked bank account

howToApply: Step 1: Visit pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC
Step 2: Register as new farmer with land and Aadhaar details
Step 3: Submit land records for verification
Step 4: After Patwari verification, payments begin

schemes.name: DAY-NULM (National Urban Livelihoods Mission)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Individual loan up to ₹2 lakh; group enterprise up to ₹10 lakh; SHG Revolving Fund ₹10,000
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — contact nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) or City Livelihood Centre
schemes.description: Reduces urban poverty through skill development, SHG formation, and credit linkage for urban poor households.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Urban poor, rag pickers, street vendors, construction workers

financial: Interest subsidy on individual loans up to ₹2 lakh
Group enterprise loan up to ₹10 lakh
nonFinancial: Street vendor support with identity cards

howToApply: Step 1: Contact nearest ULB or City Livelihood Centre
Step 2: Join SHG or apply individually for skill training
Step 3: Apply for credit linkage through ULB
Step 4: Receive training, certification, and loan

schemes.name: PMEGP for Adults (Micro Enterprise Setup)
schemes.amount: 25–45% government subsidy on project cost; project up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (services)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
schemes.description: For adults 25–45 setting up new micro enterprises with government margin money subsidy to reduce startup financial burden.

eligibility.otherCriteria: New enterprise only; minimum 8th class pass for manufacturing above ₹10 lakh

financial: Up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (services) project cost

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
Step 2: Submit project report to KVIC/KVIB/DIC
Step 3: Interview by task force
Step 4: Bank sanctions loan; subsidy credited

schemes.name: Punjab Ghar Ghar Rozgar (PGRKAM)
schemes.amount: Free job portal access + free career counseling (no direct monetary benefit)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at pgrkam.com
schemes.description: Punjab's employment portal connecting job seekers with employers through digital listings, job fairs, and career counseling.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed or underemployed graduates, diploma holders, ITI workers

nonFinancial: Free job portal with thousands of listings

howToApply: Step 1: Register at pgrkam.com
Step 2: Create profile with education and skills
Step 3: Browse jobs or register for job fairs
Step 4: Apply or attend placement camps

schemes.name: PM SVANidhi Scheme (Street Vendor Micro-Credit)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Initial ₹10,000 → ₹20,000 → ₹50,000 on timely repayment + 7% interest subsidy
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in
schemes.description: Collateral-free working capital loans for urban street vendors with escalating credit limits on timely repayment.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Certificate of Vending or ULB Letter of Recommendation; surveyed vendor

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in
Step 2: Visit nearest ULB with documents
Step 3: ULB verifies and issues Letter of Recommendation
Step 4: Apply to bank/MFI for loan

schemes.name: Sukh Samman Nidhi Scheme – Punjab (Women Support)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹1,000/month directly credited to bank account
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at sswcd.punjab.gov.in or nearest Sewa Kendra anytime
schemes.description: Monthly ₹1,000 financial support to women above 18 from low-income Punjab families to enhance financial independence.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Family income below ₹3 lakh/year; not a government employee or income tax payer

howToApply: Step 1: Apply at sswcd.punjab.gov.in or nearest Sewa Kendra/DC office
Step 2: Fill Sukh Samman Nidhi form
Step 3: Submit income proof and Aadhaar
Step 4: Monthly payment begins after verification

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Rozgar Protsahan Yojana (PMRPY)
schemes.amount: Government pays 12% employer EPF contribution for 3 years per new employee
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — employer registers new employees on EPFO portal; benefit automatic
schemes.description: Encourages employers to hire more workers by having the government pay full employer EPF contribution for new employees earning below ₹15,000/month.

eligibility.ageLimit: New employees of any age; salary below ₹15,000/month
eligibility.otherCriteria: First-time EPFO registrant; EPFO-registered employer

financial: Full employer EPF contribution (12%) paid by government for 3 years
Reduces cost of formal hiring by 12%

howToApply: Step 1: Employer registers with EPFO
Step 2: Hire new employees with salary below ₹15,000
Step 3: Register new employees on EPFO portal
Step 4: Government pays employer contribution automatically for 3 years

schemes.name: Employment Linked Incentive (ELI) – PM Viksit Bharat Rozgar Yojana
schemes.amount: Scheme A: One-month salary up to ₹15,000 for first-time employees; Scheme B: ₹3,000/month reimbursement to manufacturing employers for 2 years
schemes.deadline: Scheme approved July 2025 — implementation rolling out; check labour.gov.in for current status
schemes.description: Landmark ₹2 lakh crore employment scheme creating formal jobs for 4.1 crore youth through direct salary support and employer incentives.

eligibility.otherCriteria: First-time EPFO registrant for Scheme A; manufacturing sector for Scheme B

financial: Scheme A: One-month salary up to ₹15,000 (3 instalments) for first-time employees
Scheme B: Up to ₹3,000/month to manufacturing employers for 2 years
Scheme C: Reimbursement for employers adding 1,000+ new employees

howToApply: Step 1: First-time employees register on EPFO portal when joining formal employment
Step 2: Employer registers new employee in EPFO
Step 3: Government transfers Scheme A benefit to Aadhaar-linked account
Step 4: Manufacturing employers apply through labour ministry portal for Scheme B/C
